title: Conidial production from M. fijiensis mycelium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bso5c","text":"Continuous in vitro conidial production from Mycosphaerella fijiensis mycelium. A method for in vitro production of conidia is described. M fijiensis was grown on culture medium containing V8 juice. Mycelium was macerated until a fine suspension was produced; 2 mL was carefully spread on V8 sporulation medium. The Petri dishes were incubated for 6 days at 20 °C, under continuous cool-white fluorescent and black light. After the incubation period, 2.4 mL of 1% gelatin was added to each Petri dish, the conidia carefully dislodged with a camel hair brush. Conidial production was notably stimulated by brush harvesting mycelial cultures. After 6 days of culture the first harvest was made and the same batch of petri dishes were put in the same culture conditions to stimulate new conidial production. Significant cumulative conidial production was observed after the second harvest.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":11,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":45,"length":24},{"style":"italic","offset":93,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":138,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7tgad","text":"-V8 juice from Herdez company","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dpr95","text":"- Unflavored gelatin ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ddvc9","text":"- Camel hair brush","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"cojom","text":"1.- The mycelium colonies used for the obtention of the conidia should have before its use between 3 to 4 weeks on V8 growth medium.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4j788","text":"2.- V8 sporulation medium ready","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9389u","text":"All Petri dishes with M fijiensis should be sterilized after its last use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":22,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mix step 1 and 2 in 1 L of distiller water for 15 min 00:15:00
Measure pH and adjut to pH 6
Sterilize at 1 pressure atmosphere and 120° C for 15 min
Pour 25 mL of V8 sporulation medium into each Petri dish, wait 3 days to see if any contamination was present then it can be used

Ground mycelium using a mortar and pistile in a laminal flow cabinet cut 1 cm 2 of mycelium and put it into a mortar add 2.5 mL of destilled sterile water grind to a non-pasty liquid consistency pour 2.5 mL of the grind to each Petri dish containing V8 sporulation medium the ground mycelium is distributed making circles, until the entire Petri dish is full of suspension let the Petri dish for 20 min to dry, then seal with kleen pack

The Petri dishes were put in the folowing culture condition for sporulation Room temperature 20 °C Continuous light with cool-white fluorescent and black light (General Electric 15W/18) emitting radiant energy in the near-ultraviolet or black light spectral range Time in this conditions: for the firs harvest 5 days for the second harvest 3 days (same batch of petri dishes).

Harvest of conidia in a laminal flow cabinet Take 2400 μL of grenetine (1%) and add it to the mycelium grown in the V8S medium Sweep gently with the camel bruch Tilt the petri dish and with the help of the micropipette wash the mycelium with the same suspension After harvesting put all the suspension in a Falcon 50 mL tube, the resulting suspension must be cloudy

After the harvest take a sample for counting, if necessary you can dilute the suspension 1:10 Use the Sedgwick-Rafter chamber to count the conidia (it could be the Neubauer chamber) using a The following formula is used to calculate the concentration of conidia cells or spores/mL= ΣN x 1000 mm 3 x dilution factor A x D x F Were: N=Conidia counted A= field area 1 (mm 2 ) D= depth of field (1 mm) F= numbers of counted fields (25) 3. Once the concentration of conidia is calculated, then the concentration of the original suspension is adjusted to 200 conidia per uL 4. If the conidia are not going to be used immediately, they can be stored at 4 ° C
creator: Leticia LPE Peraza Echeverría
